stance: PARAMETERIZED FORM SCHEMAS: the compiler knows a small set of form GENERATORS (schemas), each fixing an invariant + state machine + verified lowering template + fixed op set; source instantiates schemas with types, compile-time integers, and policies drawn from CLOSED enumerations (data, never code). Writers get many concrete containers from few spec objects; the spec teaches n schemas, not n×k containers.
summary: A schema Σ is a spec object with five fixed parts: (1) a machine-checkable invariant I_Σ over instance memory (e.g. table: ctrl[i]=FULL(h) ⟺ slot i initialized with key k where h=h2(hash(k)); len ≤ 7/8·cap; cap ∈ 2^k; 16B ctrl mirror tail); (2) a parameter signature — type params with capability bounds (Copy/Movable/Droppable), integer params with declared bounds, policy params from closed finite enums, and at most bounded checked plug-points (pure xlang functions with empty write/alloc effect rows) covered by a once-proved safety-independence lemma (a wrong hasher/comparator can produce wrong ANSWERS, provably never a violation of I_Σ — exactly how SwissTable already survives bad Hash impls); (3) a fixed operation set with fixed ownership signatures and failure semantics (Option vs trap), one spelling each (R3); (4) a verified lowering template in kernel IR whose T1/T2 obligations are discharged ONCE, for all parameters, by a machine-checked proof shipped as a toolchain artifact — instantiation is substitution + monomorphization with only syntactic side-condition checks; (5) an optimizer fact sheet: invariant clauses exported at op boundaries into the existing requires-prologue engine (e.g. reserve(n) exports cap-slack(n), eliding growth/bounds checks in counted loops — the mechanism already measured at 27/27 discharged, 1.71x). The catalog is 11 schemas (seq, ring, table, btree, pool, region, bytescan, view, cursor, conc_queue, shard), 1 checked-library algorithm kit (sort/search/heap/format), 5 builtin-TCB facades (alloc, sync vocabulary, threads+par, io, rcu_table), and 2 language-primitive bundles (machine core: vectors/endian/bitops/select/prefetch; buffer core: margin_slice + wbuf). Concurrency is DRF-by-construction: all cross-thread state flows through the closed sync/queue vocabulary with affine endpoint tokens, so the spec needs publication happens-before rules, not a C11 ordering menu. Sequential schema templates are machine-verified, NOT trusted; the TCB is confined to five concurrency kernels, the allocator, io/syscall glue, intrinsic lowerings, and audited FFI records. Demonstration depth: table instantiates to hashbrown-0.15-shape (SIMD group probe, 7-bit h2 fragments, tombstone group elision, affine one-shot entry token = one hash one probe); seq instantiates to Vec/SmallVec/builder (ptr,len,cap; inline=N knob; growth ∈ {x2,x1.5}; slice view carries facts). Checker work per instantiation is O(|template|), memoized, fully syntactic.

forms.name: seq — contiguous growable sequence (schema Σ1)
forms.contract: Params: (T: Movable; inline N ≥ 0 default 0; growth ∈ {x2, x1p5}). Ops: new/with_cap; push(&uniq self, T) amortized O(1) (allocates effect; OOM traps at boundary); pop -> Option<T>; get(&self,i) -> &T checked (elidable by facts); set/get_uniq; reserve(n) exports fact cap_slack(n); truncate; view(&self) -> slice<'r,T>; view_uniq; move-out whole. Owns elements; drop is compiler-derived. Out-of-bounds = deterministic trap.
forms.perf_mechanism: (ptr,len,cap) triple, geometric growth, tail-line append = 1 predicted branch + 1 store; growth = bulk move at DRAM bandwidth; slice view gives stride-1 vectorizable iteration; inline=N puts N slots in the owner footprint (SmallVector-class malloc elimination); reserve fact + counted loop discharges the capacity check per push — Vec-class by construction, occasionally better via elision.
forms.source_shape: form Ops = seq<Op, inline=4, growth=x2>; let v = Ops.new(); Ops.reserve(&uniq v, n); loop { Ops.push(&uniq v, op); }; let s = Ops.view(&v);

forms.name: ring — single-threaded deque (schema Σ2)
forms.contract: Params: (T: Movable; growable | fixed(N)). Ops: push_back/push_front, pop_front/pop_back -> Option<T>, get(i) checked, as_slices -> (slice, slice), iteration front-to-back. Owns elements; grows by one bulk copy; no synchronization.
forms.perf_mechanism: Power-of-two buffer with masked head/tail (idx & (cap-1), 1 cycle, no branch/modulo); push/pop = one masked index update + one store/load, VecDeque-class; worklist loops stay L1-resident.
forms.source_shape: form Frontier = ring<u32, growable>; ring.push_back(&uniq q, n); match ring.pop_front(&uniq q) { some(n) => ..., none => break }

forms.name: table — open-addressing flat map/set (schema Σ3)
forms.contract: Params: (K: Movable, V: Movable (unit for set); hasher ∈ {foldhash, siphash_keyed(seed), int_identity, crc32c}; inline N ∈ {0,8,16}). Invariant I_table as in summary. Ops: insert(K,V) -> Option<V> (consumes both, returns displaced); get(&K) -> Option<&V>; get_uniq; remove(&K) -> Option<V>; entry(K) -> match occupied(&uniq V) | vacant(tok) where tok is an affine one-shot fill token; probe_view(key_view, view_eq plug) for borrowed-key lookup (interning) — no allocation on probe; iter; reserve(n) exports growth_slack(n). Plug-points (hasher instance, view_eq) are pure fns; safety-independence lemma: no plug output can index out of bounds.
forms.perf_mechanism: SwissTable template fixed: separate ctrl byte array with 7-bit h2 fragments + replicated 16B tail, 16-way SIMD group probe (movemask/NEON bridge) = filter 16 slots in ~3 instructions, triangular probe over groups, 87.5% load factor, tombstone group elision under churn; typically 2 cache lines per hit at any size; inline=N mode is a linear-scanned inline pair array until spill (SsoHashMap-class, no hash, no alloc).
forms.source_shape: form SymTab = table<span32, u32, hasher=foldhash>; match SymTab.entry(&uniq t, k) { occupied(v) => { ... }, vacant(tok) => SymTab.fill(tok, id) }

forms.name: btree — ordered map with ranges (schema Σ4)
forms.contract: Params: (K, V; key_kind ∈ {int, lex_bytes, lex_tuple, extractor-plug to one of these}). Ops: insert/get/get_uniq/remove, range(lo,hi) in-order cursor, first/last, pred/succ, ordered iter. Extractor plug is pure; invariant (node occupancy bounds, sorted separators, leaf chaining) is template-maintained regardless of plug behavior.
forms.perf_mechanism: Fixed ~256B node budget, fanout 11-16, branchless/linear in-node scan; 1M entries = ~5 levels = 5-8 line touches (vs ~20 dependent misses for RB); leaves near-contiguous so range scans run near streaming bandwidth; ~16x fewer allocations than node-per-entry trees. Rust BTreeMap / absl::btree class.
forms.source_shape: form ByDeadline = btree<u64, TaskH, key_kind=int>; let c = ByDeadline.range(&t, lo, hi); loop { match btree.next(&uniq c) { some((k,v)) => ..., none => break } }

forms.name: pool — generational slot pool / slab (schema Σ5)
forms.contract: Params: (T; mode ∈ {dense, sparse}). Handle = Copy 8-byte (idx32, gen32). Ops: insert(T) -> Handle; get(&self,h) -> match some(&T) | stale; get_uniq; remove(h) -> match some(T) | stale; iter over live slots. Slot recycling and generation bumping exist ONLY inside the verified template; gen exhaustion retires the slot (never wraps into forgeability). Stale access is a detected Option arm, never memory-unsafe.
forms.perf_mechanism: Dense slot array with in-place intrusive freelist (union of T and next-free), insert = pop head (1-2 line touches, ~2-5 ns, zero malloc); get = bounds + gen compare + indexed load (1 line, no chase); template proof that insert's constructor fully overwrites the slot ⇒ recycled slots are never re-zeroed; live iteration near Vec speed. slotmap/slab class; also the substitution substrate for pointer graphs/trees/intrusive lists.
forms.source_shape: form Conns = pool<Conn, mode=dense>; let h = Conns.insert(&uniq p, c); match Conns.get_uniq(&uniq p, h) { some(c) => ..., stale => ... }

forms.name: region — bump arena with mass free (schema Σ6, extends existing arena<'r,T>)
forms.contract: Params: (drop_mode ∈ {plain (no-drop types only), drop_at_close}; resettable flag). alloc<'r>(value) -> &'r-tied reference, heterogeneous types allowed; same-region cross-references are ordinary xlang region facts; no individual free; close frees all (running drops in drop_at_close); reset (if resettable) requires the checker's lexical proof that no 'r borrow survives, then reincarnates a fresh region id retaining chunks.
forms.perf_mechanism: Bump = add + compare + rarely-taken branch (~1-2 ns vs ~15-25 ns malloc), chunked geometric growth, zero per-object metadata, allocation-order spatial locality for tree/IR traversal; reset = pointer rewind. bumpalo/BumpPtrAllocator class; regions are already native so no lifetime gymnastics.
forms.source_shape: loop { region 'r reset(store) { let n = region.alloc<'r>(Node{...}); ... } } // store retains chunks across iterations, 'r is fresh each pass

forms.name: bytescan — block classifier / scan / fold kernel (schema Σ7)
forms.contract: Params: (pred ∈ {eq1/eq2/eq3(bytes), range_set, nibble_table(2×16B tables as DATA)}; carry ∈ {none, clmul_quote_mask, utf8_range_state}; out ∈ {first_index, last_index, all_indices, bitmask_stream, count, validate, fold(mixer ∈ {crc32_hw_3way, clmul_fold, mul_fold_xxh3, aes_mixer}, short_path)}). Runs over borrowed slice/margin_slice; returns Option index, bitmask stream, ok/first-error, or digest; no allocation; wrong tables ⇒ wrong classification only — all loads in-bounds-by-schema with masked head/tail.
forms.perf_mechanism: Fixed 64B-block pipeline: unaligned head, 4x-unrolled aligned vector loop, pshufb nibble lookups, compare-to-mask + movemask + tzcnt (NEON bridge on ARM), one-time cached ISA dispatch; zero data-dependent branches so unpredictable input costs no mispredicts; fold mode = wide loads through hw crc32/clmul/multiply mixers with a branch-minimal ≤16B path. Hosts memchr-class (20-40 GB/s), simdutf/simdjson-stage1-class, and xxh3/crc32c-class instances; the table schema's hasher enum points at fold instances.
forms.source_shape: form FindNl = bytescan<pred=eq1(0x0A), out=first_index>; match FindNl.run(hay) { some(i) => ..., none => ... } // catalog ships utf8_validate, json_stage1, base64, ws_skip, xxh3, crc32c as named instances

forms.name: view — validated typed overlay on borrowed bytes (schema Σ8)
forms.contract: Param: a layout declaration L (fields with byte offset, width, endianness, alignment; fixed-size or length-prefixed sections). open<L>(bytes) -> Option<view<'r,L>> performs the one-time length/shape validation and records it as a fact; field reads/writes thereafter are proven residual-check-free; view lifetime tied to the buffer region; malformed input = none, never a fault.
forms.perf_mechanism: Post-validation field access = exactly 1 (possibly unaligned) load + optional bswap — zero bounds or validity branches, bit-identical to a C packed-struct dereference (zerocopy-class, verifiable by asm diff); packet header / page cell stays in 1-2 lines.
forms.source_shape: layout TcpHdr = { src: u16 be @0, dst: u16 be @2, seqno: u32 be @4, ..., size = 20 }; match view.open<TcpHdr>(pkt) { some(h) => { let p = view.get(h, src); }, none => ... }

forms.name: cursor — checked stream decoder over (margin) bytes (schema Σ9)
forms.contract: Params: (margin W ∈ {0,8,16,32}; streams ∈ {1,2,4} for interleaved bit-readers). Affine cursor threaded through ops: take_u8/u16/u32/u64(endian), varint_u64, take_slice(n), bits_refill/peek/consume per stream; over margin_slice<W> inputs, wide loads past the logical end are legal into the slop; ops return (cursor', value) or a truncation error arm. Includes lz_copy(dst: wbuf/margin out, offset, len): verified overlap-tolerant copy (offset < len legal) with wild vector stores into guaranteed writable slop. Malformed input = error arm, never corruption.
forms.perf_mechanism: Varint = one unaligned 8B load + shift/mask (no 1-10 iteration byte loop); bounds amortized: margin makes per-op over-read checks vanish, requires-facts amortize remaining checks to per-field/per-message; 2-4 interleaved bitstreams break serial refill dependency (3-4x ILP, zstd-class); lz_copy = 1-2 vector stores per match with no length loop or per-copy branch (LZ4-class 4-5 GB/s).
forms.source_shape: let c0 = cursor.open<margin=8>(buf); let (c1, tag) = cursor.varint_u64(c0)?; let (c2, body) = cursor.take_slice(c1, n)?;

forms.name: conc_queue — bounded concurrent FIFO family (schema Σ10)
forms.contract: Params: (T: Movable + Send-capability; endpoints ∈ {spsc, mpmc, steal}; cap = 2^k). new() returns endpoint TOKENS: spsc -> (producer, consumer) both affine non-shareable (uniqueness compile-time nonforgeable); mpmc -> shareable handle; steal -> affine owner + shareable stealer. push(&uniq p, T) -> ok | full(T) (value returned — affine-safe backpressure); pop -> some(T) | empty; steal -> stolen(T) | empty | retry. Ownership transfers through the queue exactly once; kernels are trusted records behind this safe facade.
forms.perf_mechanism: spsc: Lamport-refined — padded per-line counters, single-writer-per-counter plain+release stores, cached opposing index ⇒ zero RMW, ~0.1 atomic ops/item batched (rtrb/DPDK class). mpmc: Vyukov per-slot sequence numbers ⇒ exactly one RMW/op, publication local to the slot line (crossbeam ArrayQueue class). steal: Chase-Lev — owner push/pop with no RMW (one SeqCst fence on pop, CAS only on last-element race), stealers CAS the top (crossbeam-deque class, the par runtime substrate).
forms.source_shape: form LogQ = conc_queue<Msg, endpoints=spsc, cap=1024>; let (p, c) = LogQ.new(); /* move p, c into threads */ match LogQ.push(&uniq p, m) { ok => ..., full(m) => backoff }

forms.name: shard — sharded concurrency wrapper (schema Σ11)
forms.contract: Params: (F = another schema instance, typically table; k with 2^k shards; hasher tied to F's). Ops: enter(&self, key) -> affine guard token exposing &uniq F-instance for the shard (guard drop = unlock, lexical); ops on the inner instance go through the guard; weakly-consistent iteration by shard. One level of schema composition only (no shard<shard<...>>).
forms.perf_mechanism: Hash bits pick 1 of 2^k mutex-protected inner instances: contention confined to 1/2^k of traffic; uncontended shard lock = one uncontested CAS (~10-15 ns) + plain SwissTable probe. dashmap-class: ~1.5-2x single-thread cost per op, near-linear mixed scaling to 16 threads.
forms.source_shape: form Sessions = shard<table<u64, Sess, hasher=siphash_keyed>, k=6>; let g = Sessions.enter(&s, id); Sessions.inner_insert(&uniq g, id, sess);

forms.name: alg kit — sort / search / heap / number formatting
forms.contract: sort(uniq slice; stable ∈ {yes,no}; key_kind as btree) — O(n log n) worst case, near-O(n) on sorted runs; partition_point/binary_search over sorted slices; implicit 4-ary heap over a seq (push/pop_min/peek); itoa/ryu-class digit emission into the seq<u8> builder via the emit contract. All ordinary checked xlang source over schema forms — inside the safety proof, no TCB.
forms.perf_mechanism: Sort: ipnsort/driftsort-class comparison core whose branchless partition is built on the guaranteed select form (no compiler wobble), plus key_kind-driven auto-dispatch to LSB radix for u32/u64 at large n (voracious-class, ~2.5x the ipnsort bar); search: cmov-guaranteed probe (offset += (probe<key)*half); heap: 4-ary flat array halves height and packs 4 children per line; itoa: 2-digit LUT ~4 ns/u64 vs ~100 ns snprintf. Bounds inside all loops discharged by seq/slice facts.
forms.source_shape: alg.sort(&uniq v_view, stable=no, key_kind=int); let i = alg.partition_point(s, probe_env); heap.push(&uniq h, (key, val));

forms.name: sync vocabulary — mutex/once/counter/publish/condvar
forms.contract: mutex<T>: lock(&self) -> affine lexical guard exposing &uniq T (lock-protects-data); condvar wait/notify on a mutex; once<T>: racing init, one winner, all see initialized; counter: relaxed fetch_add/load u64; publish<T>/snapshot: release-store a frozen (immutable, Send) value, acquire-load a snapshot view — the read-mostly substitute for rwlock. This closed vocabulary is the ONLY cross-thread shared-state surface; spec gives publication happens-before, not a C11 ordering menu. DRF-by-construction preserves T1 (races unrepresentable).
forms.perf_mechanism: Word-sized futex adaptive mutex inline with data (~10-20 ns uncontended roundtrip, parking_lot-class, zero syscalls uncontended); once check = 1 acquire load + predicted branch (~1 ns); counter = one locked RMW (~2-7 ns uncontended); publish/snapshot read = ~2 ns acquire load writing NO shared lines (arc-swap-class linear read scaling); native acq/rel on ARM so no blanket SeqCst tax.
forms.source_shape: let g = mutex.lock(&state); table.insert(mutex.inner(&uniq g), k, v); /* guard drops at scope end */ let cfg = publish.load(&cfg_cell);

forms.name: alloc — global allocator facade
forms.contract: malloc/free/realloc semantics consumed implicitly by every allocating schema; cross-thread free supported; no writer-visible internals; n=1 for all consumers. Authoring a NEW allocator in-language is explicitly out of catalog (TCB-adjacent capability), a candid residue.
forms.perf_mechanism: mimalloc-class trusted kernel: per-thread heaps, size-class free lists embedded in pages, fast path ~10-20 instructions / 1-2 lines / zero atomics, remote frees batched onto one atomic-exchange list; ~15-25 ns pair, mimalloc-bench-class scaling.
forms.source_shape: invisible: box.new / seq / table growth route through it; no user-facing calls.

forms.name: threads & par — scoped spawn, worker pool, parallel-for
forms.contract: spawn_scoped<'r>(env) — thread may borrow 'r data; checker proves join dominates 'r scope end (lexical); join returns value or propagates failure trap; persistent pool amortizes spawn; thread_local<T> lazy per-thread state (no atomics on access); par.for_chunks(uniq slice, chunk, worker-env-struct) and par.join(a_env, b_env) with the disjoint-split rule proving chunk disjointness; deterministic reduce via checked-law contracts (assoc/identity refuted at compile time if false).
forms.perf_mechanism: Chase-Lev substrate (Σ10 steal) ⇒ ~20-50 ns amortized task overhead, geometric splitting makes steals log-rare, parallel sum saturates socket DRAM bandwidth (rayon-class); TLS access = one segment-relative load + predicted init branch; spawn ~15 us amortized to ~0 by the pool.
forms.source_shape: par.for_chunks(&uniq data, 4096, SumEnv{ acc }); // env struct + contract impl, monomorphized (P5); no closures

forms.name: io facade — streams, sockets, completion ring, filemap, WAL, os services
forms.contract: io.stream: buffered read/write with fill_buf borrow-the-buffer (parse in place), explicit flush, O_DIRECT aligned mode; io.net: nonblocking sockets in an event loop, vectored writev, buffer reuse via wbuf, REUSEPORT sharding, recvmmsg/GSO sub-facade; io.ring: completion-style submit-batch/await — each in-flight op holds a LINEAR buffer-loan token (buffer inaccessible until completion returns it; nonforgeable); io.filemap: read-only frozen file map -> immutable bytes with owned SIGBUS discipline (lock-or-copy default, defined trap otherwise); io.wal: durable append log (group commit, checksummed frames) + atomic-file-replace + low-level escape (fdatasync/dirsync/order); io.os: posix_spawn processes, vDSO clocks, buffered CSPRNG entropy, shutdown signal hook. Errors are explicit result arms; traps only at declared boundaries.
forms.perf_mechanism: 64-256KB buffering amortizes ~300-600 ns syscalls to ~0.01 ns/byte with zero-copy in-buffer parsing; io_uring lowering drops syscalls/op below 1 (epoll emulation elsewhere at mio-class); warm map access = pure loads, zero syscalls/copies; WAL group commit turns fsync-per-txn into fsync-per-batch (~100x commit throughput); posix_spawn ~60 us independent of parent RSS; vDSO clock ~25 ns.
forms.source_shape: let (buf_view, n) = io.fill_buf(&uniq f)?; ...; io.consume(&uniq f, n); let tok = io.ring.submit_read(&uniq ring, fd, wbuf_loan); match io.ring.await1(&uniq ring) { done(tok, wbuf_back, n) => ... }

forms.name: rcu_table — read-mostly concurrent map
forms.contract: get from any thread with zero shared-line writes; insert/remove serialized internally; per-key get-or-insert; weakly-consistent snapshot iteration. Shipped as a sealed builtin (a table instance under the internal epoch-reclamation kernel); NO user-facing epoch/hazard API exists — reclamation is hidden exactly as 99% of C++/Rust users already consume it.
forms.perf_mechanism: Epoch pin = thread-local store + compiler fence (~1-3 ns, no shared RMW) ⇒ linear read scaling where RwLock<HashMap> plateaus at 2-3 threads; deferred batched frees amortize reclamation below 1 ns/read (papaya/folly-class).
forms.source_shape: form Routes = rcu_table<u64, Route>; match Routes.get(&r, k) { some(v) => ..., none => ... } // v is a pinned-scope view

forms.name: machine core — vectors, endian/align, bit ops, select, prefetch
forms.contract: Width-explicit vector value types (u8x16..u64x8) with a CLOSED op/intrinsic enumeration (lane ops, shuffles/pshufb-table, compare-to-mask, movemask bridge, clmul, aes-round) + one-time cached ISA dispatch declaration; endian-explicit loads/stores at arbitrary offsets (lower to 1 mov + optional bswap); align(N) and cachepad attributes on types/allocations; integer methods popcount/tzcnt/lzcnt/rotate/pdep/pext with guaranteed single-instruction lowering + a set-bit iteration loop form; select(cond, a, b) with two strictness levels — hint (compiler may branch when profitable) and strict (verified never-branch, constant-time); prefetch(addr) with no-op semantics (never faults, never observable, no effect row) and an nt flag on blessed copy/fill.
forms.perf_mechanism: These ARE the machine mechanisms F5/F9 par depends on: 16-64 lanes/instruction, movemask+tzcnt scalar bridge in 2 instructions, penalty-free unaligned access with split-line avoidance via alignment, 64B padding killing false sharing (5-20x on contended counters), cmov eliminating ~15-20-cycle mispredicts on 50/50 data, 8-16 in-flight prefetches unlocking full memory-level parallelism (2-4x DRAM-resident probe loops), NT stores skipping read-for-ownership on huge fills.
forms.source_shape: let m = u8x32.eq(chunk, splat(0x0A)); let idx = mask.tzcnt(u8x32.movemask(m)); let x = load_be_u32(bytes, off); let lo = select.strict(p, a, b); prefetch(table_addr(h2));

forms.name: buffer core — margin_slice and wbuf
forms.contract: margin_slice<'r, T, W>: a slice carrying a STATIC guarantee of W extra readable (and, for outputs, writable) slop elements past its logical end; produced only by allocation-side forms (seq.reserve_margin, buffer_with_margin, copy-in) — never forgeable by narrowing an ordinary slice; consumed by cursor/bytescan for over-reads and by lz_copy for wild writes. wbuf<'r>: write-cursor buffer with type-tracked initialized extent — kernel/decoder writes advance a monotonic committed prefix; freeze exposes exactly the written prefix as initialized bytes; reading past the commit point is unrepresentable; zero memset in the path by construction.
forms.perf_mechanism: margin_slice deletes per-op tail checks and length loops (varint 8B loads, vector tails without masks, LZ wild copies = the 2-4x class of codec wins); wbuf deletes the double-store pass (64KB memset ~2 us + cache eviction, 20-50% of small-read syscall paths) matching C read()-into-uninitialized and Rust BorrowedBuf.
forms.source_shape: let mb: margin_slice<'r, u8, 8> = seq.view_margin<8>(&buf); let w = wbuf.new(64*1024); let (w2, n) = io.read_into(&uniq f, w)?; let bytes = wbuf.freeze(w2, n);

forms.name: ffi boundary records
forms.contract: extern-C declarations as toolchain-gated, human-approved trusted records: C-ABI function signatures over repr(C)-declared types, pointer+len crossings with stated ownership/lifetime/aliasing contracts in the record, callback exposure; the record class is the constitution's ONLY trusted class; ordinary source can neither declare nor widen one. Calls compile to a bare ABI call.
forms.perf_mechanism: Machine code identical to a C-to-C call (~1-2 ns: arguments in ABI registers, no marshaling, no pinning, no thread-state bookkeeping); buffers cross as pointer+len because layout is bit-compatible — the Rust extern-C bar, explicitly not the cgo ~40-60 ns anti-target.
forms.source_shape: trusted_record ffi Blas { fn dgemm(ptr f64, len, ...) contract { reads(a[0..n]), writes(c[0..m]), no-retain }; } // record file signed + audited, referenced from source by name

language_deltas: Schema objects + instantiation judgment: a new spec object class Σ = (invariant, parameter signature, op table, verified lowering template, fact sheet); judgment ⊢ inst(Σ, args) OK checks closed-enum membership, integer bounds, and type capability bounds syntactically, then monomorphizes the template — no per-instance re-verification.

Plug-point admission judgment: a schema parameter may be a user function only if its effect row is pure (no writes/allocates/traps beyond declared) AND the schema carries a once-proved safety-independence lemma stating I_Σ holds for arbitrary plug outputs; wrong plugs yield wrong answers, never unsafety.

Fact-sheet export rule: schema ops may emit invariant clauses (cap_slack(n), growth_slack(n), validated(L), sorted(view)) into the existing requires-prologue fact engine; every exported fact class is a fact channel requiring hostile review before shipping.

margin_slice<'r,T,W> type: slice with static W-element readable/writable slop past logical end; introduction only at allocation-side forms (reserve_margin, buffer_with_margin), elimination only by margin-consuming ops (over-read loads, wild stores) whose obligations are discharged by W; no narrowing/forging rule.

wbuf<'r> init-extent type: affine write-cursor whose committed prefix grows monotonically within a function (linear cursor threading, no flow analysis); freeze(w, n ≤ committed) is the single point exposing initialized bytes; reads of uncommitted bytes are untypeable.

Endpoint tokens + Send/share capabilities: conc_queue/io.ring endpoints are affine non-Copy tokens (existing affine machinery) with a new shareable-handle capability marker for mpmc/stealer handles; a Send capability bound restricts which T may cross threads.

Minimal memory model (publication semantics): all cross-thread shared state flows through the closed sync/queue vocabulary; spec adds happens-before edges only at those ops (release-publish/acquire-read, channel transfer, mutex acquire/release, join); programs are data-race-free by construction, so sequential reasoning remains valid everywhere else — no user-facing ordering menu.
syncs effect-row entry: ops that establish happens-before carry a syncs effect so the optimizer knows fence points and never migrates memory ops across them.
Scoped thread spawn rule: spawn_scoped<'r> may capture 'r borrows iff its join lexically dominates the end of 'r; checked with existing lexical-region machinery.

Disjoint uniq-slice split rule: a structural judgment splitting one uniq slice view into k non-overlapping uniq chunk views (by index arithmetic on constants/lengths), founding par.for_chunks with zero aliasing inference.

Region reincarnation rule: reset on a resettable region requires no live 'r borrows (lexical), then issues a fresh region identifier while retaining backing chunks — arena reuse across loop iterations with all dangling references untypeable.

Vector value types + closed intrinsic enumeration + ISA dispatch declaration: fixed-width SIMD values as ordinary Copy scalars-of-lanes; ops drawn from a closed spec list; one dispatch declaration per program resolves ISA level once at startup.

Endian-explicit load/store forms and align(N)/cachepad attributes: load_le/load_be/store_* at arbitrary byte offsets on byte views, guaranteed 1 load/store + at most 1 bswap; alignment attributes on types and allocations.

Single-instruction bit-op methods + set-bit iteration loop form: popcount/tzcnt/lzcnt/rotate/pdep/pext with guaranteed lowering; a blessed loop form iterating set bits (tzcnt + x&=x-1) so the idiom has one spelling.

select form with lowering-guarantee judgment: select.hint (optimizer may branch when profitable) and select.strict (verified branch-free lowering, constant-time; verification is a codegen-side check, not writer trust).
prefetch hint + nt store flag: prefetch(addr) is semantically a no-op (never faults, no observable effect, empty effect row); blessed copy/fill primitives take an nt policy flag.

Layout declaration sublanguage + validation fact: 'layout' declarations (field offset/width/endianness/alignment, length-prefixed sections) compiled by the view schema; validated(L, bytes) is a fact making subsequent field accesses residual-check-free.

FFI boundary record judgment: trusted_record ffi declarations form the toolchain-gated trusted class — signed, human-approved, carrying ownership/aliasing/lifetime contracts; ordinary source may reference but never define or widen them.

coverage.scenario: Growable contiguous vector (append-heavy owned buffer)
coverage.how: Σ1 seq is (ptr,len,cap) with x2/x1.5 growth: tail-line push, bulk-move growth, stride-1 slice view; reserve exports cap_slack(n) so counted push loops carry zero residual capacity checks (existing requires engine, measured mechanism). Vec-class by construction.

coverage.scenario: Small/inline vector (allocation-count-dominated short sequences)
coverage.how: Same Σ1 with inline=N knob (absl InlinedVector shape): N slots in the owner footprint, transparent heap spill; eliminates the malloc/free pair and keeps 2-8-element lists on the owner's lines — SmallVector-class on the millions-of-tiny-lists workload.

coverage.scenario: String/byte builder (append-only formatting and serialization buffer)
coverage.how: Σ1 seq<u8> plus the emit contract; alg-kit itoa/ryu-class LUT digit kernels (~4 ns/u64, checked source) and bulk append lowering to memcpy after a reserve fact. snprintf-class is structurally impossible since there is no format-string path.

coverage.scenario: Sorting owned runs (comparison and key-based)
coverage.how: alg-kit sort: ipnsort/driftsort-class core whose branchless partition is built on the GUARANTEED select form (no LLVM cmov wobble), run detection for near-O(n) sorted input, stable flag; key_kind lets the single blessed entry point auto-dispatch to LSB radix on u32/u64 at large n — voracious-class ~2.5x the preregistered ipnsort bar on integer keys, matching it elsewhere.

coverage.scenario: Binary search / lookup over sorted contiguous runs
coverage.how: alg-kit partition_point with select.strict guaranteeing the cmov probe (offset += (probe<key)*half); operates on the seq slice view so layout is caller-controlled; Eytzinger is a taught layout transform over the same op for giant frozen tables.

coverage.scenario: General hash map/set (unordered point lookup, medium-to-large n)
coverage.how: Σ3 table is the SwissTable template verbatim: ctrl-byte array with 7-bit h2 + 16B mirror, 16-way SIMD group probe, triangular probing, 87.5% load, tombstone group elision; entry is an affine one-shot vacant token (one hash, one probe); hashers from the closed fold-kernel enum. hashbrown-class ~2 lines/hit; chained-map shape is unrepresentable in the catalog.

coverage.scenario: Tiny map/set (n <= ~8-16, allocation- and hash-cost dominated)
coverage.how: Σ3 inline=N mode: inline (K,V) pair array linear-scanned until spill (SsoHashMap/SmallDenseMap hybrid) — no hashing, no allocation, 1-2 lines at n≤8; a knob on the map, not a second shape.

coverage.scenario: Ordered map with range queries (B-tree class)
coverage.how: Σ4 btree: ~256B nodes, fanout 11-16, branchless in-node scan, chained leaves; range/pred/succ in the fixed op set; key kinds closed (int, lex bytes, lex tuples, extractor plug). Rust BTreeMap/absl class; RB-tree shape not offered.

coverage.scenario: Bounded cache with eviction (LRU class)
coverage.how: Taught composition, no new primitive: Σ3 table (key -> handle) + Σ5 pool slab holding entries with embedded u32 prev/next recency fields; promote = 2-4 stores on already-fetched lines; CLOCK/S3-FIFO variants are link-discipline changes in the same pattern. Passes the map's own composition test.

coverage.scenario: Interning / deduplication (value -> stable small id)
coverage.how: Σ6 region byte store (bump append) + Σ3 probe_view op — borrowed key-view probe against arena-owned spans with zero allocation (the map capability the demand map names as the real requirement) + Σ1 span/id vector. rustc-interner shape exactly; resolve = one indexed load.

coverage.scenario: Concurrent hash map (shared read-mostly or sharded-write lookup)
coverage.how: Mixed workloads: Σ11 shard over Σ3 (dashmap-class: uncontended shard CAS + Swiss probe, near-linear to 16 threads). Read-mostly: rcu_table builtin-TCB with epoch readers writing zero shared lines (papaya-class linear read scaling). The reclamation mechanism is sealed inside the builtin.

coverage.scenario: Bump arena (mass-free allocation for build-then-drop object graphs)
coverage.how: Σ6 region: add+compare bump (~1-2 ns), chunked growth, mass free at close; same-arena cross-references are native lexical-region facts (the hard checker part already exists in xlang's region system); reset mode reincarnates the region id retaining chunks.

coverage.scenario: Slab/pool with generational handles (stable identity + individual free)
coverage.how: Σ5 pool: in-place freelist, 8-byte Copy (idx32,gen32) handles, get -> some/stale. Recycling lives ONLY inside the verified template (answers the prior well-typed-UAF objection to writer-rolled freelists); generation exhaustion retires the slot so stale handles are never forgeable into live ones. slotmap-class insert ~3-5 ns, get ~1-2 ns.

coverage.scenario: Graphs (CFGs, dependency graphs, meshes) via index adjacency
coverage.how: Taught two-phase pattern over existing forms: build = Σ1 adjacency seqs over u32 ids; freeze to CSR = two Σ1 seqs (offsets + packed edges); payloads in SoA parallel arrays (already the project's P2 idiom). Same bytes moved as petgraph CSR — bandwidth math identical.

coverage.scenario: Trees with parent pointers / cross-links (navigable node soups)
coverage.how: Σ5 pool + u32 parent/child/sibling link fields as plain data (16B header): detach/reattach = 3-5 index stores, zero allocator traffic. Multi-node mutation under no-reborrow uses copy-out the Copy links, then one get_uniq at a time (or a command buffer) — extra source discipline, identical machine code to indextree/ego-tree/rowan.

coverage.scenario: Intrusive lists / membership links (O(1) unlink from within the element)
coverage.how: Embedded u32 prev/next fields inside pool elements; unlink = 2-4 stores to lines the caller already fetched; multi-list membership = multiple link-field pairs; splice moves state without rehash. The irreducible residue — links threaded through FREE memory, mimalloc-style — lives only inside the allocator TCB, matching the demand map's own verdict.

coverage.scenario: Deque / FIFO work buffer (single-threaded)
coverage.how: Σ2 ring: power-of-two masked head/tail over a growable buffer — one masked index update + one store/load per op, growth by single bulk copy, as_slices exposed. VecDeque-class; chunked-deque and linked shapes are not in the catalog.

coverage.scenario: SPSC ring buffer (two-thread streaming handoff)
coverage.how: Σ10<spsc>: Lamport-refined kernel — cache-line-padded monotonic counters, single writer per counter (zero RMW), cached opposing index (~0.1 atomics/item batched), wait-free both sides. Producer/consumer are affine tokens, so endpoint uniqueness is compile-time nonforgeable — same machine code as rtrb with stronger cheat-proofing.

coverage.scenario: MPMC bounded queue (many-to-many channel buffer layer)
coverage.how: Σ10<mpmc>: Vyukov per-slot sequence-number kernel (trusted record behind the safe facade) — exactly one RMW per op, slot-local publication; push -> ok | full(T) returns the value on backpressure, which fits affine ownership cleanly. crossbeam ArrayQueue class.

coverage.scenario: Priority queue (heap) and the timer specialization
coverage.how: Heap: alg-kit implicit 4-ary heap over Σ1 (pure checked source; bounds elided via facts; 4 children per line halves lines touched per sift). Timers: taught wheel pattern — array of Σ5-pool buckets with embedded links + masked index, O(1) insert/cancel. Decrease-key served by lazy deletion, per the map.

coverage.scenario: Work-stealing deque (per-worker task queue with thief end)
coverage.how: Σ10<steal>: Chase-Lev trusted kernel (paper-grade proof + exhaustive small-bound model checking) behind affine owner token + shareable stealer handles; owner push/pop with no RMW and the one SeqCst fence; also the substrate the par runtime is built on. Exactly the once-proved-by-experts disposition the demand map prescribes.

coverage.scenario: Single/few-byte scanning (memchr class)
coverage.how: Σ7 bytescan<eq1..eq3|range, out=first/last/all>: fixed 64B-block pipeline — unaligned head, 4x-unrolled vector loop, compare+movemask+tzcnt, masked tail, one-time ISA dispatch. The memchr-crate shape is the template; short-input path included; reverse variant is a direction flag.

coverage.scenario: Block-parallel validation and structural indexing (simdjson/simdutf class)
coverage.how: Σ7 with nibble_table predicates (tables are DATA) + closed carry policies (clmul quote mask, Keiser-Lemire utf8 range state) + bitmask_stream output; catalog ships utf8_validate, json_stage1, base64, ws_skip as named instances. Novel kernels beyond the closed vocabulary fall to the F9 vector core (still checked source) — the schema covers the named SOTA uses.

coverage.scenario: Serialization/deserialization of structured messages
coverage.how: (a) POD blit: Σ8 view + Copy = memcpy-class; (b) varint/TLV: Σ9 cursor over margin_slice — one unaligned 8B load per varint, bounds amortized by margin + facts, encode into reserved seq<u8> tail; (c) offset-graph zero-copy: view+cursor composition with once-validated offset facts — protobuf/prost-class solid, full capnp-generality is the thinnest part of the claim.

coverage.scenario: Checksums and non-cryptographic hashing
coverage.how: Σ7 fold output mode: closed mixer set {crc32-hw 3-stream, clmul_fold, mul_fold (xxh3-class), aes_mixer} + branch-minimal ≤16B short path; both demand profiles (short-key latency, bulk throughput) are instances; Σ3's hasher enum consumes these same instances so the map's probe cost inherits them.

coverage.scenario: Compression codec inner loops (LZ + entropy)
coverage.how: Exactly the two blessed sub-shapes the demand map says suffice, both designed here: margin_slice output + Σ9 lz_copy (verified overlap-tolerant wild copy — 1-2 vector stores into guaranteed slop, no per-copy length branch, offset<len legal) and the Σ9 multi-stream bit reader (2-4 interleaved streams over margin input). Encoder match-finder is a Σ3 instance. Par is contingent on these two ops hitting their templates — flagged in risks.

coverage.scenario: Zero-copy typed views over binary layouts (packets, pages, records)
coverage.how: Σ8 view: layout declarations with per-field offset/width/endianness/alignment; open() validates once and records the fact; every subsequent field access is proven residual-check-free = 1 load (+bswap), C-parity verifiable by asm diff. Data-dependent internal offsets compose with Σ9 cursor + once-proven bounds facts, as the map prescribes.

coverage.scenario: General-purpose allocator fast path (mimalloc/jemalloc class)
coverage.how: Builtin-TCB mimalloc-class kernel behind the n=1 facade; all schemas route through it; fast path ~10-20 instructions, zero atomics, remote-free batching. Honest residue: AUTHORING a new allocator in-language is out of catalog (needs links through freed memory and type-changing reuse) — consumption is par, authorship stays TCB-adjacent, matching the map's own split.

coverage.scenario: Arena / bump allocation with scope-lifetime free
coverage.how: Σ6 region with the two modes the map names: plain-data (no-drop types) vs drop_at_close; reset-and-reuse via the region-reincarnation rule (checker proves no surviving borrows lexically, chunks retained). bumpalo-class ~1-3 ns/alloc with construction-order locality.

coverage.scenario: Slot pools / slabs: O(1) reuse without re-zeroing
coverage.how: Σ5 pool: freelist pop/push at 1-2 line touches; NO re-zeroing because the verified template proves insert's constructor fully overwrites the slot before any read is typeable — the safety proof, not a memset, guarantees no stale-byte reads; dense storage iterates at streaming bandwidth.

coverage.scenario: Uninitialized buffers: zero-init avoidance with tracked init extent
coverage.how: wbuf language primitive: monotonic committed prefix tracked in the type, kernel/decoder writes advance it, freeze exposes only written bytes; reads of uncommitted memory are untypeable, so the memset disappears by proof — BorrowedBuf-class, C codegen parity (zero zeroing instructions in the I/O path).

coverage.scenario: Memory-mapped data: file-backed byte regions
coverage.how: Read-only frozen maps: io.filemap -> immutable bytes + Σ8 views, SIGBUS owned by the facade (lock-or-copy default, defined-trap option) — LMDB-read-path par. MISSING: shared MUTABLE maps (external writer mutating pages) — breaks the single-process aliasing proof and needs a volatile-external-bytes contract with per-access/per-snapshot discipline that this design defers rather than fakes.

coverage.scenario: Portable SIMD vector types with runtime ISA dispatch
coverage.how: Language-primitive vector types with a CLOSED op/intrinsic enumeration including the named non-portable essentials (pshufb-table, clmul, aes-round, movemask bridge) + one-time cached dispatch — one form plus the named-intrinsic escape inside the same type system, exactly the map's resolution; Σ7 is implemented in it, so par is demonstrated wherever Σ7 hits its bars.

coverage.scenario: Unaligned loads, endianness, and alignment control
coverage.how: Endian-explicit load/store forms guaranteed to lower to 1 mov (+1 bswap for BE) on byte views; align(N) on types/allocations for vector-width and page alignment; cachepad attribute giving contended atomics their own 64B line (the 5-20x false-sharing fix). The map's n=1 scenario, delivered as n=1.

coverage.scenario: Word-level bit operations: popcount/ctz/clz/pdep-pext and set-bit iteration
coverage.how: Integer method set with guaranteed single-instruction lowering plus the blessed set-bit iteration loop form (tzcnt + x&=x-1, one spelling); pdep/pext carry the pre-Zen3 dispatch fallback as an implementation detail. Σ3's probe loop consumes exactly these, so hashbrown-class lookup inherits them.

coverage.scenario: Branchless selection and predication (cmov class)
coverage.how: select.hint / select.strict with a verified lowering guarantee: strict mode is machine-checked never-to-branch (constant-time capable). C/C++/Rust have no supported way to FORCE cmov short of inline asm, and LLVM's cmov decisions wobble across versions — a guaranteed select removes an entire class of silent performance regressions in sort/search/crypto kernels. Concrete Rust-test advantage in cheat-proofness of the lowering.

coverage.scenario: Software prefetch and non-temporal memory streams
coverage.how: prefetch(addr) as a semantically-no-op hint (never faults, empty effect row — safe by hardware construction) usable in ordinary loops, so group-prefetch batching is just a scheduling idiom; nt flag on the blessed copy/fill primitives for huge write-once regions. No new shape, per the map.

coverage.scenario: Thread lifecycle: spawn/join, worker pools, thread-local state
coverage.how: spawn_scoped<'r> with the lexical join-dominates-region rule (borrowed spawns, crossbeam::scope-class); builtin persistent pool amortizes ~15 us spawns to ~0; thread_local lazy form (1 predicted init branch) for writers, with raw initial-exec TLS reserved to runtime/allocator internals — exactly the split the map says is par for all consumers.

coverage.scenario: Atomic cells and the memory-ordering vocabulary
coverage.how: Closed idiom vocabulary — counter (relaxed fetch_add), publish/snapshot (release/acquire publication of frozen values), cas_retry form — covering the ~95% stereotyped uses; DRF-by-construction means no user-facing six-ordering menu; lowering uses native acq/rel so ARM pays no blanket SeqCst tax. Lock/queue implementer residue is absorbed into shipped kernels, per the map.

coverage.scenario: Mutual exclusion: mutex, rwlock, one-time init
coverage.how: Builtin mutex<T> word-sized futex adaptive (parking_lot-class ~10-20 ns uncontended), guard is a lexical affine token so lock-protects-data is structural; once<T> ~1 ns post-init; condvar integrated. Read-mostly hot data is deliberately routed to publish/snapshot instead of an rwlock — adopting the map's finding that rwlock is not par there.

coverage.scenario: Queues and channels: SPSC ring vs MPMC channel
coverage.how: Σ10 spsc/mpmc as above plus a blocking channel facade with futex parking; the map's own unification hypothesis — 'plausible in a language with linear endpoint types' — is exactly what the affine endpoint tokens deliver: one schema, endpoint policy chosen by type, SPSC's zero-RMW path preserved because uniqueness is proven, not assumed.

coverage.scenario: Concurrent keyed state: sharded maps and read-mostly tables
coverage.how: Σ11 shard over Σ3 for mixed workloads (dashmap-class); rcu_table builtin for reader-writes-nothing linear scaling (folly/papaya-class); shard-by-thread idiom composes from thread_local + Σ3 with no new form. The two blessed shapes the map says suffice.

coverage.scenario: Safe memory reclamation (epoch/hazard/RCU class)
coverage.how: The hide-it disposition, taken deliberately: the epoch kernel is an internal trusted record consumed by rcu_table and any future shipped lock-free forms; NO user-facing reclamation API. Consumers get crossbeam-epoch-class read costs invisibly. Users cannot author novel lock-free linked structures — an explicit catalog boundary handled by the findings process, not a silent gap.

coverage.scenario: Data parallelism: parallel-for / fork-join with work stealing
coverage.how: par builtin: fork-join + for_chunks over the disjoint uniq-slice split rule (aliasing disproved structurally, no inference), Chase-Lev substrate, env-struct workers (P5, no closures), deterministic reduce via the checked-law contracts (assoc/identity machine-refuted if false). rayon-class ~25-50 ns task overhead and bandwidth-saturating scans.

coverage.scenario: Buffered sequential file I/O
coverage.how: io.stream: 64-256KB buffering, fill_buf borrow-the-buffer parse-in-place (zero second copy), explicit flush, precise error arms; O_DIRECT + alignment mode for engine authors as the map's second form. BufReader/stdio-class amortization by construction.

coverage.scenario: Network sockets: TCP/UDP request paths
coverage.how: io.net: nonblocking sockets in the event loop, vectored writev merging header+body, TCP_NODELAY/cork discipline, REUSEPORT per-core sharding, steady-state zero allocation via wbuf reuse; recvmmsg/GSO as the UDP sub-facade. Kernel-bypass (DPDK/XDP) excluded from the small catalog, per the map.

coverage.scenario: Readiness/completion multiplexing (epoll/kqueue/io_uring/IOCP class)
coverage.how: io.ring: completion-style submit-batch/await facade lowering to io_uring where available, epoll+nonblocking emulation elsewhere (the libuv/tokio-proven portability layer); the in-flight buffer is a LINEAR loan token — inaccessible until completion returns it — making the map's 'buffer loaned to the kernel' ownership fact nonforgeable at the type level.

coverage.scenario: mmap and shared memory
coverage.how: Read-only private maps blessed (frozen bytes + views + owned SIGBUS discipline); anonymous hugepage-aligned reservations hidden inside the allocator TCB. MISSING: writable MAP_SHARED IPC — an external writer defeats single-process aliasing proofs; needs an atomic/volatile access discipline over shared pages that this design defers explicitly rather than claiming.

coverage.scenario: Durability ordering: fsync discipline and write-ahead logging
coverage.how: io.wal durable-append-log form (append frames, group commit, checksummed frames, one fdatasync per batch) + atomic-file-replace form (temp, fsync, rename, dirsync) + the small low-level escape (per-fd fdatasync, directory sync, ordering control) for custom engines — the map's two-forms-plus-escape verdict implemented as such.

coverage.scenario: FFI call boundary (calling and being called by C)
coverage.how: extern-C boundary records: bare ABI call (~1-2 ns, cgo-class overhead structurally impossible), repr(C) layout on declared crossing types, pointer+len buffers. Trust is contained exactly as the constitution prescribes: records are the toolchain-gated, human-approved trusted class, auditable in one place, never definable by ordinary source.

coverage.scenario: Process and OS services: spawn, time, entropy, signals
coverage.how: io.os facade: posix_spawn-backed process launch (~60 us independent of parent RSS), vDSO clocks (~25 ns), getrandom-seeded userspace CSPRNG buffer (~ns per draw), shutdown-signal hook. All differentiating choices live inside the facade — the map's clearest n=1 scenario, kept at n=1.

tcb: Added trusted surface, exactly: (1) five concurrency kernels — SPSC counter protocol, Vyukov MPMC, Chase-Lev deque, epoch reclamation (internal to rcu_table and future shipped lock-free forms), and the futex mutex/parking + thread/TLS bootstrap — shipped as toolchain-gated trusted records with paper-grade human-approved proofs PLUS exhaustive small-bound model checking (loom/GenMC-style) as CI gates, sealed behind facades whose affine/shareable token types make misuse unrepresentable; (2) the global allocator internals (mimalloc-class: links through freed memory, type-changing block reuse — modeled as out-of-language); (3) io facade glue: syscall wrappers, io_uring ring ABI, mmap + SIGBUS discipline, signal/entropy/clock paths; (4) ISA intrinsic lowerings and the one-time dispatch machinery; (5) per-program extern-C boundary records (audited, signed, the constitution's only trusted class); (6) as today, the compiler backend and the proof-artifact checker. Critically NOT in the TCB: all eleven sequential schema templates (seq, ring, table, btree, pool, region, bytescan, view, cursor, the shard wrapper logic, and every alg-kit routine) — each template's T1/T2 obligations are discharged once, for all parameter values, by a machine-checked proof against its invariant I_Σ; the proof artifact ships with the toolchain and is re-verified by a small checker at toolchain build. Each schema's optimizer fact sheet is a fact channel and receives hostile adversarial review before shipping, per standing rule. Net TCB delta is dominated by the five concurrency kernels + allocator + io glue — a bounded, enumerable list with named verification stories, versus zero user-extensible trust.
checker_story: The checker's new decisions are all local and syntax-directed. Per instantiation it decides: (a) legality — policy args are members of closed enums, integer args within declared bounds, type args satisfy declared capability bounds (Copy/Movable/Send) — pure syntactic checks; (b) plug-point admission — the supplied hasher/extractor/eq function has the required pure effect row, using the existing effect-row machinery; (c) nothing else: the template's safety proof is a pre-verified toolchain artifact, so instantiation is substitution + monomorphization at O(|template|) cost, memoized per parameter tuple. Schema ops are then ordinary function calls with fixed ownership signatures — the existing affine machinery handles entry tokens, guards, endpoints, and cursors without extension, since they are just affine non-Copy values. Fact-sheet consumption reuses the shipped requires-prologue engine verbatim (facts are the same class of boundary facts it already discharges). Only three genuinely new judgments enter the kernel: margin arithmetic (W is a compile-time constant; over-read/over-write obligations are discharged by constant comparison), wbuf's monotonic commit (an affine cursor threaded in ANF — a per-function linear scan, no dataflow analysis), and disjoint uniq-slice split (structural index arithmetic on constants and lengths). Regions, scoped spawn, and reset reuse the existing lexical-region system. There are no fixpoints, no inference, no inter-procedural analysis; every rejection is deterministic with a named rule, preserving the settled frontend-simple feasibility ruling.
ai_writability: A mid-tier writer's workflow is: find the scenario row in the taught decision table (keyed by this demand map: 'owned sequence -> seq; keyed lookup -> table; ordered ranges -> btree; stable identity + churn -> pool; build-then-drop graph -> region; two-thread stream -> conc_queue<spsc>; ...'), write ONE instantiation line with concrete types and enum policies, then use the fixed op set — each op has exactly one spelling, fixed ownership arrows, and match-shaped failure arms the exhaustiveness checker forces them to handle. Nothing invariant-bearing is writable: a writer cannot mis-size a control group, forget a generation bump, or misorder a fence, because those live in verified templates or trusted kernels; the worst a wrong plug-point can do is a wrong answer. Misparameterization is a compile error that enumerates the closed choices, which is the friendliest possible repair signal for a model. The worst teaching hazard is the no-reborrow discipline around stateful forms: multi-step interactions (entry tokens, shard guards, cursor threading, mutating one pool node while navigating from another) require the affine-token idioms, and a modest model that fights the borrow-consumption rule will drift into copy-out/clone-shaped code that compiles, stays safe, and silently loses the performance the catalog exists to deliver — par decays without any error appearing. Mitigations: the spec teaches each idiom as the ONLY spelled shape with a worked example per schema, and checker diagnostics on borrow-consumption errors name the blessed idiom to use ('use entry(k) instead of get then insert'). The second hazard, wrong-form selection (ring vs seq vs pool), is mechanical given the decision table and is exactly what the catalog-finding process monitors.
risks: Template verification is the load-bearing bet: mechanically proving SwissTable/B-tree-grade templates safe against I_Σ for all parameters is serious verification work; if it stalls, templates must be reclassified as trusted records, silently multiplying the TCB by ~10 spec objects — preregister E1's proof-closure deadline as the tripwire.

Spec growth: +~48 rules on a 90-rule kernel strains D2; the design survives only if schema op tables and policy enums genuinely live in catalog text rather than kernel rules — if each schema leaks 5+ rules into the kernel, the compactness constraint is breached.

Closed policy enums may be too closed: the first real workload needing an out-of-enum policy (robin-hood probing, a custom hasher, a new carry state for bytescan) forces catalog churn through the findings process; if such findings arrive weekly rather than quarterly, the closed-vocabulary premise is wrong.

Minimal memory model sufficiency: publication-only happens-before must be proven adequate for every shipped kernel's facade contract; any kernel whose safe interface leaks an ordering subtlety to users reopens the C11 menu the design explicitly excludes.

bytescan generality vs par: simdjson-stage1-class kernels may not decompose into the closed predicate/carry vocabulary at par on all ISAs (NEON lacks movemask; the bridge costs differ) — the schema may deliver 0.7x rather than 1.0x on ARM, demoting F5-2 to PARTIAL.

Codec forms (lz_copy, multi-stream bit reader) are designed but unproven: overlapping self-referential copies with offset<len are the single hardest verified-template obligation in the catalog; failure here concedes the map's predicted 2-4x loss on compression.
Monomorphization blowup: many (K,V,policy) tuples multiply template stampings — compile time and code size need the memoization plus an instantiation-count budget in CI.

Pool generational recycling re-decides the prior anti-recycling ruling (P2's never-recycled indices); it is safe only because recycling lives inside a verified template, but this needs owner ratification and hostile review of generation-exhaustion semantics before shipping.

Fact sheets are fact channels: an unsound exported fact under an exotic parameter combination is an optimizer-visible soundness hole; every schema's fact sheet needs per-parameter-space hostile review, not just template review.

No-reborrow ergonomics: if mid-tier models systematically fall off the affine-token idioms into copy-out patterns, measured par decays in the field with no compile-time signal; needs a lint that flags copy-out-then-insert shapes adjacent to blessed idioms.
first_experiments: E1 (table schema, the keystone): implement the Σ3 template in kernel IR, machine-verify its memory-safety obligations against I_table (all loads/stores in-bounds, no read of non-FULL slots, for ALL K,V,hasher,N parameters), then instantiate table<u64,u64,foldhash> and benchmark against hashbrown 0.15 + foldhash on the same machine: 1M uniform-random u64 keys, DRAM-resident, 95%-hit lookup and insert-with-growth. PASS: proof closes within 4 person-weeks and the artifact re-checks in < 60 s at toolchain build, AND lookup ns/op ratio vs hashbrown ∈ [0.90, 1.15], insert-with-growth ∈ [0.90, 1.25], AND frontend instantiation cost ≤ 50 ms. FAIL: any band missed, or proof not closable — which reclassifies templates as TCB and demands a redesign decision before proceeding.

E2 (seq schema + fact channel): instantiate seq<u64> and seq<u64, inline=4>; benchmark (a) 1M-push-then-sum vs Rust 1.80 Vec, (b) the compiler workload: 10M cycles of build-a-4-element-list-then-drop, inline=4 vs inline=0. PASS: (a) total runtime ∈ [0.95, 1.10]x Vec, asm inspection shows the post-reserve counted push loop contains ZERO residual capacity/bounds branches (fact-elision working) and the sum loop is vectorized packed adds; (b) inline=4 ≥ 3.0x faster than inline=0 (allocation elimination dominating, SmallVector-class). FAIL: outside either band, or any residual per-push check survives the reserve fact.

E3 (bytescan schema breadth): instantiate memchr (pred=eq1, out=first_index) and utf8_validate (nibble_table + utf8_range_state carry) from the SAME Σ7 template on an AVX2 machine; benchmark vs memchr crate 2.7 and simdutf. PASS: memchr instance ≥ 0.80x memchr-crate GB/s on 16KB rare-match haystacks AND ≤ 2.0x its latency on 8-byte inputs; utf8_validate ≥ 0.70x simdutf GB/s on both ASCII-heavy and mixed-multibyte corpora; both instances share one verified template with only data/policy differences (diff of stamped IR confirms no per-instance hand code).
